ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Nov-16-2015 Continued To Feb-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Terese Raddie for (trustee) Alvin Chin. Cathy Nelson for (beneficiary) Lester Chin.

The Court asked if the parties had mediated. Ms. Nelson reported that they recently agreed to mediation with JAMS, that they hope to narrow the scope for trial, that they want a 90 day continuance and that if the matter is not resolved at that time they will set a date for trial. The accounting petitions, the bifurcated issues and the petition to approve amended accountings were continued to February 29, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-09-2015 Continued To Jun-22-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kathy Nelson for (petitioner & beneficiary) Lester Chin. Terese Raddie for (trustee) Alvin Chin.

Ms. Nelson reported that the remainder beneficiaries are having problems trying to figure out how the Millbrae rental and the restaurant properties income and expenses are paid, everything is done in accounts outside of the trust and they need to see records for both starting in March of 2010. Ms. Raddie reported that the restaurant account is in joint tenancy outside of the trust, that the Millbrae account was never in the trust, that those accounts do not need to be accounted for, that they have followed the courts order regarding accountings, that these accounts have never been in her clients possession and that obtaining the documents and providing an accounting would be an added expense. Ms. Raddie also reported that a declaration has been filed by Ms. Hughes stating that the key to the Massachusetts safe deposit box was available, that Ms. Hughes does not want to provide an inventory as others have accessed the box, and that Ms. Hughes wants the box changed into the name of the trustee. Ms. Raddie stated that she will file an amended accounting to include the 2 accounts and the assets in Massachusetts. Ms. Nelson stated that they had previously requested their attorney's fees to be paid by the trustee, or in the alternative by the trust. The two accounting petitions and the bifurcated issues were continued to June 22, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the amended petition to be filed with time for notice. Ms. Raddie is to prepare an order regarding the upcoming dates.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Accounting
Off Calendar Jun-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Cathy Nelson for Lester Chin. Terese Raddie for Alvin Chin (Trustee).

The Court granted Petition to Compel Accounting (subject to review of the order). The time period for the accounting is to be determined. The remaining issues are bifurcated and continued to July 30, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the accounting is also set for July 30, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
